title: noallia incarnata
intro: Noallia incarnata, also known as Blumea incana, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Noallia incarnata is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts for medicinal use.
